growth_records.medium: Chemically defined medium (CDM) for Lactococcus lactis and L. cremoris of Poolman and Konings (cited ref 30); custom growth-media file in Table S6

up.method: reported (cited from ref. 41, 'near maximum specific growth rate on glucose under anaerobic conditions')

growth_records.cite: (GEM methods paper; value cited from ref. 41) et al. High-throughput generation and comparison of genome-scale metabolic models reveal strain-specific metabolic diversity in 439 Lactococcus strains. mSystems (2025). DOI:10.1128/msystems.01517-25. [PMC13098203]
growth_records.snippet: L. cremoris MG1316, 24 mmol g-1 h-1 near maximum specific growth rate on glucose under anaerobic conditions (ref 41)
growth_records.notes: Number explicit in this GEM paper's Methods, attributed to an external cited study (ref. 41), not generated by this paper. No medium/temperature/pH details for the original measurement given in this paper's text. [metadata completed from paper Methods: medium]

growth_records.medium: CDMPC (chemically defined medium for prolonged cultivation) + fructose

sec.method: L. cremoris is homolactic; lactic acid production per biomass measured (Fig 6G) but no absolute rate stated

product_yields.ATP_per_fructose: 2 ATP per sugar molecule (reported energetic yield; low growth yield)

growth_records.cite: Ekkers et al. Trade-Offs Predicted by Metabolic Network Structure Give Rise to Evolutionary Specialization and Phenotypic Diversification. Molecular Biology and Evolution (2022). DOI:10.1093/molbev/msac124. [PMC9206417]
growth_records.snippet: dairy strains of L. cremoris exhibit a relatively high growth rate (0.82 h−1; supplementary fig. S3, Supplementary Material online) but low growth yield (two ATP per sugar molecule) when growing on fructose
growth_records.notes: Ancestral L. cremoris MG1363 on fructose. mu=0.82 h-1 reported (max growth rate, CDMPC+fructose; supplementary fig. S3). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.82 = 0.845 h. Energetic yield 2 ATP/fructose reported (not a biomass yield). CDMPC composition not given in text (referenced Goel/Ekkers et al. 2019) → medium_composition_stated=false. Bioreactor conditions: 30 °C, pH 6.5, anaerobic (N2); phenotype growth-rate measurement semianaerobic microplate at 30 °C. Homolactic fermenter (lactate main product) but no absolute secretion flux reported.

growth_records.medium: CDMPC (chemically defined medium for prolonged cultivation) + galactose

sec.method: homolactic; lactic acid per biomass measured (Fig 6G) but no absolute rate stated

product_yields.ATP_per_galactose: 3 ATP per sugar molecule (reported energetic yield; high growth yield)

growth_records.cite: Ekkers et al. Trade-Offs Predicted by Metabolic Network Structure Give Rise to Evolutionary Specialization and Phenotypic Diversification. Molecular Biology and Evolution (2022). DOI:10.1093/molbev/msac124. [PMC9206417]
growth_records.snippet: L. cremoris growing on galactose as the sole carbon source exhibit high growth yield (three ATP per sugar molecule) but a low growth rate (0.43 h−1; supplementary fig. S3, Supplementary Material online)
growth_records.notes: Ancestral L. cremoris MG1363 on galactose. mu=0.43 h-1 reported (max growth rate, CDMPC+galactose; supplementary fig. S3). doubling_time = ln2/mu = 0.6931/0.43 = 1.612 h. Energetic yield 3 ATP/galactose reported (not biomass yield). CDMPC composition not stated (referenced) → medium_composition_stated=false. Conditions 30 °C, pH 6.5, anaerobic bioreactor / semianaerobic microplate assay. NOTE: evolved specialists reached group-average midpoint growth rates F=0.703 and G=0.545 h-1 (Fig 3), but these are demarcation midpoints of clustered dataset averages, not single-strain measurements, so not emitted as separate records.

phenotypes.base_medium: Enzyme / activity assay (hydrolysis, degradation, reduction, electron transfer) in a test medium — not a sole-substrate growth test
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 14691 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/14691

phenotypes.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)
phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 14691 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/14691

phenotype_groups.label: Complex/peptone base (acid-production test)
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Acid/gas-production test: peptone/complex base + substrate + pH indicator — positive = fermentative acid production, NOT growth on the substrate as a sole carbon source (interpret with care for model validation)

phenotype_groups.label: Defined minimal medium (sole-substrate growth)
phenotype_groups.base_medium: Sole-substrate growth / assimilation on a defined minimal medium (substrate as C/N/energy source) — positive = the organism grows on it

carbon_growth.medium: CDMPC (chemically defined medium for prolonged cultivation) + fructose
carbon_growth.citation: Ekkers et al. Trade-Offs Predicted by Metabolic Network Structure Give Rise to Evolutionary Specialization and Phenotypic Diversification. Molecular Biology and Evolution (2022). D
